6  Social Network Analysis in R

From Networks to Insights

Author

Dr Clemens Jarnach

Published

March 16, 2026

7 Setup and Essentials

Loading packages

library(igraph)
library(tidygraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidyverse)
library(sna)

Core R concepts

Before working with networks, we need a solid grasp of R fundamentals.

Basic operations and assignment

1 + 3           # evaluation
[1] 4
a <- 9          # assignment
sqrt(a)         # function application
[1] 3
b <- sqrt(a)
a == b          # logical comparison
[1] FALSE
ls()            # objects in environment
[1] "a" "b"

Vectors

x <- c(1, 3, 5)        # combine operator
x[2]                   # indexing
[1] 3
y <- c("one", "three", "five")

# Sequences
a <- 1:5
b <- seq(from = 1, to = 5, by = 1)
identical(a, b)
[1] FALSE
# Vectorised operations
x > 2
[1] FALSE  TRUE  TRUE
any(x > 2)
[1] TRUE
all(x > 2)
[1] FALSE
which(x > 2)
[1] 2 3

Matrices

m <- matrix(1:25, nrow = 5, ncol = 5)
m[1, 2]                 # element access
[1] 6
m[1, ]                  # row
[1]  1  6 11 16 21
m[, 2]                  # column
[1]  6  7  8  9 10
m[2:3, 3:5]             # submatrix
     [,1] [,2] [,3]
[1,]   12   17   22
[2,]   13   18   23
# Matrix operations
m %*% t(m)              # matrix multiplication
     [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5]
[1,]  855  910  965 1020 1075
[2,]  910  970 1030 1090 1150
[3,]  965 1030 1095 1160 1225
[4,] 1020 1090 1160 1230 1300
[5,] 1075 1150 1225 1300 1375
t(m)                    # transpose
     [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5]
[1,]    1    2    3    4    5
[2,]    6    7    8    9   10
[3,]   11   12   13   14   15
[4,]   16   17   18   19   20
[5,]   21   22   23   24   25
dim(m)
[1] 5 5

Data frames

df <- data.frame(
  income = 1:5,
  sane = c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE),
  name = LETTERS[1:5],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

df$sane                 # dollar notation
[1]  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE FALSE
df[1, 2]
[1] TRUE
df[-1, ]                # negative indexing
  income  sane name
2      2  TRUE    B
3      3  TRUE    C
4      4  TRUE    D
5      5 FALSE    E

8 Importing and Creating Networks

Built-in network data

data(flo)               # Medici family network
flo
             Acciaiuoli Albizzi Barbadori Bischeri Castellani
Acciaiuoli            0       0         0        0          0
Albizzi               0       0         0        0          0
Barbadori             0       0         0        0          1
Bischeri              0       0         0        0          0
Castellani            0       0         1        0          0
Ginori                0       1         0        0          0
Guadagni              0       1         0        1          0
Lamberteschi          0       0         0        0          0
Medici                1       1         1        0          0
Pazzi                 0       0         0        0          0
Peruzzi               0       0         0        1          1
Pucci                 0       0         0        0          0
Ridolfi               0       0         0        0          0
Salviati              0       0         0        0          0
Strozzi               0       0         0        1          1
Tornabuoni            0       0         0        0          0
             Ginori Guadagni Lamberteschi Medici Pazzi Peruzzi
Acciaiuoli        0        0            0      1     0       0
Albizzi           1        1            0      1     0       0
Barbadori         0        0            0      1     0       0
Bischeri          0        1            0      0     0       1
Castellani        0        0            0      0     0       1
Ginori            0        0            0      0     0       0
Guadagni          0        0            1      0     0       0
Lamberteschi      0        1            0      0     0       0
Medici            0        0            0      0     0       0
Pazzi             0        0            0      0     0       0
Peruzzi           0        0            0      0     0       0
Pucci             0        0            0      0     0       0
Ridolfi           0        0            0      1     0       0
Salviati          0        0            0      1     1       0
Strozzi           0        0            0      0     0       1
Tornabuoni        0        1            0      1     0       0
             Pucci Ridolfi Salviati Strozzi Tornabuoni
Acciaiuoli       0       0        0       0          0
Albizzi          0       0        0       0          0
Barbadori        0       0        0       0          0
Bischeri         0       0        0       1          0
Castellani       0       0        0       1          0
Ginori           0       0        0       0          0
Guadagni         0       0        0       0          1
Lamberteschi     0       0        0       0          0
Medici           0       1        1       0          1
Pazzi            0       0        1       0          0
Peruzzi          0       0        0       1          0
Pucci            0       0        0       0          0
Ridolfi          0       0        0       1          1
Salviati         0       0        0       0          0
Strozzi          0       1        0       0          0
Tornabuoni       0       1        0       0          0

Importing relational data

# Read adjacency matrix
# relations <- read.csv("relationalData.csv", header = FALSE)
# relations <- as.matrix(relations)

# Read vertex attributes
# nodeInfo <- read.csv("vertexAttributes.csv", header = TRUE)

# Assign row/column names
# rownames(relations) <- nodeInfo$name
# colnames(relations) <- nodeInfo$name

Creating network objects with igraph

The igraph package is now the standard for network analysis in R. It’s faster, more flexible, and better maintained than older packages.

# From adjacency matrix
g_flo <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(flo, mode = "undirected", weighted = NULL)

# Quick inspection
g_flo
IGRAPH 7073e08 UN-- 16 20 -- 
+ attr: name (v/c)
+ edges from 7073e08 (vertex names):
 [1] Acciaiuoli--Medici       Albizzi   --Ginori      
 [3] Albizzi   --Guadagni     Albizzi   --Medici      
 [5] Barbadori --Castellani   Barbadori --Medici      
 [7] Bischeri  --Guadagni     Bischeri  --Peruzzi     
 [9] Bischeri  --Strozzi      Castellani--Peruzzi     
[11] Castellani--Strozzi      Guadagni  --Lamberteschi
[13] Guadagni  --Tornabuoni   Medici    --Ridolfi     
[15] Medici    --Salviati     Medici    --Tornabuoni  
+ ... omitted several edges
# Create from edge list
edges <- tibble(
  from = c(1, 1, 2, 2, 3),
  to   = c(2, 3, 3, 4, 4)
)

g <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE)
g
IGRAPH 9672bb2 UN-- 4 5 -- 
+ attr: name (v/c)
+ edges from 9672bb2 (vertex names):
[1] 1--2 1--3 2--3 2--4 3--4
# Empty network
g_empty <- make_empty_graph(n = 5, directed = FALSE)

9 Network Description and Visualization

Basic network properties

vcount(g_flo)                  # number of vertices
[1] 16
ecount(g_flo)                  # number of edges
[1] 20
is_directed(g_flo)
[1] FALSE
is_weighted(g_flo)
[1] FALSE

Static visualisation

Base igraph plotting

plot(g_flo, 
     vertex.size = 8,
     vertex.label.cex = 0.8,
     edge.width = 1.5)

ggraph for publication-quality plots

ggraph(g_flo, layout = 'fr') +
  geom_edge_link(edge_width = 0.5, edge_colour = 'grey70') +
  geom_node_point(size = 5, colour = 'steelblue') +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_graph()

10 Working with Edges and Vertices

Adding and removing edges

g <- make_empty_graph(n = 5)

# Add edges by indexing
g <- g + edge(c(1, 2, 2, 3))
g
IGRAPH c35a8dc D--- 5 2 -- 
+ edges from c35a8dc:
[1] 1->2 2->3

Vertex and edge attributes

# Add vertex attributes
V(g_flo)$degree <- igraph::degree(g_flo)
V(g_flo)
+ 16/16 vertices, named, from 7073e08:
 [1] Acciaiuoli   Albizzi      Barbadori    Bischeri    
 [5] Castellani   Ginori       Guadagni     Lamberteschi
 [9] Medici       Pazzi        Peruzzi      Pucci       
[13] Ridolfi      Salviati     Strozzi      Tornabuoni  
# Add edge attributes (on weighted example)
g_weighted <- g_flo
E(g_weighted)$weight <- runif(ecount(g_flo))

11 Centrality Measures

Degree centrality

deg <- igraph::degree(g_flo)
deg
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
           1            3            2            3            3 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
           1            4            1            6            1 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
           3            0            3            2            4 
  Tornabuoni 
           3

Betweenness centrality

bet <- igraph::betweenness(g_flo)
bet
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
    0.000000    19.333333     8.500000     9.500000     5.000000 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
    0.000000    23.166667     0.000000    47.500000     0.000000 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
    2.000000     0.000000    10.333333    13.000000     9.333333 
  Tornabuoni 
    8.333333

Closeness centrality

clo <- igraph::closeness(g_flo)
clo
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
  0.02631579   0.03448276   0.03125000   0.02857143   0.02777778 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
  0.02380952   0.03333333   0.02325581   0.04000000   0.02040816 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
  0.02631579          NaN   0.03571429   0.02777778   0.03125000 
  Tornabuoni 
  0.03448276

Eigenvector centrality

eig <- igraph::eigen_centrality(g_flo)$vector
eig
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
   0.3071155    0.5669336    0.4919853    0.6572037    0.6019551 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
   0.1741141    0.6718805    0.2063449    1.0000000    0.1041427 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
   0.6407743    0.0000000    0.7937398    0.3390994    0.8272688 
  Tornabuoni 
   0.7572302

Visualising centrality

ggraph(g_flo, layout = 'fr') +
  geom_edge_link(edge_width = 0.5, edge_colour = 'grey70') +
  geom_node_point(aes(size = deg), colour = 'steelblue', alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 8)) +
  theme_graph() +
  labs(size = "Degree")

12 Network-Level Indices

Density and clustering

edge_density(g_flo)
[1] 0.1666667
transitivity(g_flo)              # global clustering coefficient
[1] 0.1914894

Reciprocity (directed networks)

# Create a directed example
g_dir <- graph_from_data_frame(
  tibble(from = c(1, 2, 2, 3), to = c(2, 1, 3, 2)),
  directed = TRUE
)

reciprocity(g_dir)
[1] 1

Components and connectivity

igraph::components(g_flo)
$membership
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
           1            1            1            1            1 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
           1            1            1            1            1 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
           1            2            1            1            1 
  Tornabuoni 
           1 

$csize
[1] 15  1

$no
[1] 2
is_connected(g_flo)
[1] FALSE
# Geodesic distances
distances(g_flo)[1:3, 1:3]
           Acciaiuoli Albizzi Barbadori
Acciaiuoli          0       2         2
Albizzi             2       0         2
Barbadori           2       2         0
diameter(g_flo)
[1] 5

13 Subgroups and Cohesion

k-cores

kc <- coreness(g_flo)
kc
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
           1            2            2            2            2 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
           1            2            1            2            1 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
           2            0            2            1            2 
  Tornabuoni 
           2

Cliques

cliques(g_flo, min = 3)
[[1]]
+ 3/16 vertices, named, from 7073e08:
[1] Medici     Ridolfi    Tornabuoni

[[2]]
+ 3/16 vertices, named, from 7073e08:
[1] Castellani Peruzzi    Strozzi   

[[3]]
+ 3/16 vertices, named, from 7073e08:
[1] Bischeri Peruzzi  Strozzi

Communities (modularity-based)

comm <- cluster_louvain(g_flo)
membership(comm)
  Acciaiuoli      Albizzi    Barbadori     Bischeri   Castellani 
           1            2            3            3            3 
      Ginori     Guadagni Lamberteschi       Medici        Pazzi 
           2            2            2            1            1 
     Peruzzi        Pucci      Ridolfi     Salviati      Strozzi 
           3            4            1            1            3 
  Tornabuoni 
           1 
modularity(comm)
[1] 0.39875
ggraph(g_flo, layout = 'fr') +
  geom_edge_link(edge_width = 0.5, edge_colour = 'grey70') +
  geom_node_point(aes(colour = factor(membership(comm))), size = 5) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_graph() +
  theme(legend.position = 'bottom')

14 Structural Equivalence

# Correlation of profiles
se_dist <- similarity(g_flo, method = "invlogweight")

# Hierarchical clustering
hc <- hclust(as.dist(1 - se_dist))
plot(hc)

15 Random Graph Models

Erdős–Rényi

Each possible edge is included independently with probability p. Produces a network with roughly uniform randomness and low clustering.

g_er <- sample_gnp(n = 20, p = 0.15)

plot(g_er, main = "Erdős–Rényi (Gnp)", vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "gray")

Configuration model (fixed degree sequence)

Generates a random graph that preserves a specified degree sequence. Useful to test network properties while controlling for node degrees.

g_conf <- igraph::sample_degseq(c(3,3,2,2,2))
plot(g_conf, main = "Configuration Model", vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "steelblue")

Small-world (Watts–Strogatz)

Starts with a regular lattice and randomly rewires edges with probability p. Produces networks with high clustering and short path lengths.

g_ws <- sample_smallworld(dim = 1, size = 50, nei = 2, p = 0.1)
plot(g_ws, main = "Small-World (Watts–Strogatz)", vertex.size = 5, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "orange")

Scale-free (preferential attachment)

Nodes are added one by one, preferentially attaching to high-degree nodes. Produces networks with hubs and a power-law degree distribution.

g_sf <- sample_pa(n = 50, power = 1, directed = FALSE)
plot(g_sf, main = "Scale-Free (Preferential Attachment)", vertex.size = 6, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "purple")

16 Further Resources

  • igraph documentation: ?igraph, https://igraph.org/r/
  • ggraph: Extension for publication-quality plots
  • tidygraph: Working with networks in the tidyverse
  • sna: Classical functions (older but still useful)
5  R Basics
References

# Setup and Essentials

## Loading packages

```{r}
#| message: false
#| warning: false


library(igraph)
library(tidygraph)
library(ggraph)
library(tidyverse)
library(sna)
```

## Core R concepts

Before working with networks, we need a solid grasp of R fundamentals.

### Basic operations and assignment

```{r}
1 + 3           # evaluation
a <- 9          # assignment
sqrt(a)         # function application
b <- sqrt(a)
a == b          # logical comparison
ls()            # objects in environment
```

### Vectors

```{r}
x <- c(1, 3, 5)        # combine operator
x[2]                   # indexing
y <- c("one", "three", "five")

# Sequences
a <- 1:5
b <- seq(from = 1, to = 5, by = 1)
identical(a, b)

# Vectorised operations
x > 2
any(x > 2)
all(x > 2)
which(x > 2)
```

### Matrices

```{r}
m <- matrix(1:25, nrow = 5, ncol = 5)
m[1, 2]                 # element access
m[1, ]                  # row
m[, 2]                  # column
m[2:3, 3:5]             # submatrix

# Matrix operations
m %*% t(m)              # matrix multiplication
t(m)                    # transpose
dim(m)
```

### Data frames

```{r}
df <- data.frame(
  income = 1:5,
  sane = c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE),
  name = LETTERS[1:5],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

df$sane                 # dollar notation
df[1, 2]
df[-1, ]                # negative indexing
```

---

# Importing and Creating Networks

## Built-in network data

```{r}
data(flo)               # Medici family network
flo
```

## Importing relational data

```{r}
# Read adjacency matrix
# relations <- read.csv("relationalData.csv", header = FALSE)
# relations <- as.matrix(relations)

# Read vertex attributes
# nodeInfo <- read.csv("vertexAttributes.csv", header = TRUE)

# Assign row/column names
# rownames(relations) <- nodeInfo$name
# colnames(relations) <- nodeInfo$name
```

## Creating network objects with igraph

The `igraph` package is now the standard for network analysis in R. It's faster, more flexible, and better maintained than older packages.

```{r}
# From adjacency matrix
g_flo <- graph_from_adjacency_matrix(flo, mode = "undirected", weighted = NULL)

# Quick inspection
g_flo
```

```{r}
# Create from edge list
edges <- tibble(
  from = c(1, 1, 2, 2, 3),
  to   = c(2, 3, 3, 4, 4)
)

g <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE)
g
```

```{r}
# Empty network
g_empty <- make_empty_graph(n = 5, directed = FALSE)
```

---

# Network Description and Visualization

## Basic network properties

```{r}
vcount(g_flo)                  # number of vertices
ecount(g_flo)                  # number of edges
is_directed(g_flo)
is_weighted(g_flo)
```

## Static visualisation

### Base igraph plotting

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
plot(g_flo, 
     vertex.size = 8,
     vertex.label.cex = 0.8,
     edge.width = 1.5)
```

### ggraph for publication-quality plots

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
ggraph(g_flo, layout = 'fr') +
  geom_edge_link(edge_width = 0.5, edge_colour = 'grey70') +
  geom_node_point(size = 5, colour = 'steelblue') +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_graph()
```

---

# Working with Edges and Vertices

## Adding and removing edges

```{r}
g <- make_empty_graph(n = 5)

# Add edges by indexing
g <- g + edge(c(1, 2, 2, 3))
g


```

## Vertex and edge attributes

```{r}
# Add vertex attributes
V(g_flo)$degree <- igraph::degree(g_flo)
V(g_flo)
# Add edge attributes (on weighted example)
g_weighted <- g_flo
E(g_weighted)$weight <- runif(ecount(g_flo))
```

---

# Centrality Measures

## Degree centrality

```{r}
deg <- igraph::degree(g_flo)
deg
```

## Betweenness centrality

```{r}
bet <- igraph::betweenness(g_flo)
bet
```

## Closeness centrality

```{r}
clo <- igraph::closeness(g_flo)
clo
```

## Eigenvector centrality

```{r}
eig <- igraph::eigen_centrality(g_flo)$vector
eig
```

## Visualising centrality

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
ggraph(g_flo, layout = 'fr') +
  geom_edge_link(edge_width = 0.5, edge_colour = 'grey70') +
  geom_node_point(aes(size = deg), colour = 'steelblue', alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  scale_size(range = c(2, 8)) +
  theme_graph() +
  labs(size = "Degree")
```

---

# Network-Level Indices

## Density and clustering

```{r}
edge_density(g_flo)
transitivity(g_flo)              # global clustering coefficient
```

## Reciprocity (directed networks)

```{r}
# Create a directed example
g_dir <- graph_from_data_frame(
  tibble(from = c(1, 2, 2, 3), to = c(2, 1, 3, 2)),
  directed = TRUE
)

reciprocity(g_dir)
```

## Components and connectivity

```{r}
igraph::components(g_flo)
is_connected(g_flo)
```
```{r}
# Geodesic distances
distances(g_flo)[1:3, 1:3]
diameter(g_flo)
```

---

# Subgroups and Cohesion

## k-cores

```{r}
kc <- coreness(g_flo)
kc
```

## Cliques

```{r}
cliques(g_flo, min = 3)
```

## Communities (modularity-based)

```{r}
comm <- cluster_louvain(g_flo)
membership(comm)
modularity(comm)
```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6
ggraph(g_flo, layout = 'fr') +
  geom_edge_link(edge_width = 0.5, edge_colour = 'grey70') +
  geom_node_point(aes(colour = factor(membership(comm))), size = 5) +
  geom_node_text(aes(label = name), repel = TRUE, size = 3) +
  theme_graph() +
  theme(legend.position = 'bottom')
```

---

# Structural Equivalence

```{r}
# Correlation of profiles
se_dist <- similarity(g_flo, method = "invlogweight")

# Hierarchical clustering
hc <- hclust(as.dist(1 - se_dist))
plot(hc)
```

---

# Random Graph Models

## Erdős–Rényi
Each possible edge is included independently with probability p. Produces a network with roughly uniform randomness and low clustering.
```{r}
g_er <- sample_gnp(n = 20, p = 0.15)

plot(g_er, main = "Erdős–Rényi (Gnp)", vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "gray")
```

## Configuration model (fixed degree sequence)
Generates a random graph that preserves a specified degree sequence. Useful to test network properties while controlling for node degrees.
```{r}
g_conf <- igraph::sample_degseq(c(3,3,2,2,2))
plot(g_conf, main = "Configuration Model", vertex.size = 20, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "steelblue")
```

## Small-world (Watts–Strogatz)
Starts with a regular lattice and randomly rewires edges with probability p. Produces networks with high clustering and short path lengths.

```{r}
g_ws <- sample_smallworld(dim = 1, size = 50, nei = 2, p = 0.1)
plot(g_ws, main = "Small-World (Watts–Strogatz)", vertex.size = 5, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "orange")
```

## Scale-free (preferential attachment)
Nodes are added one by one, preferentially attaching to high-degree nodes. Produces networks with hubs and a power-law degree distribution.

```{r}
g_sf <- sample_pa(n = 50, power = 1, directed = FALSE)
plot(g_sf, main = "Scale-Free (Preferential Attachment)", vertex.size = 6, vertex.label = NA, edge.color = "purple")

```




---

# Further Resources

- **igraph documentation**: `?igraph`, `https://igraph.org/r/`
- **ggraph**: Extension for publication-quality plots
- **tidygraph**: Working with networks in the tidyverse
- **sna**: Classical functions (older but still useful)
